_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Mucocolpos, hematocolpos, or pyohematocolpos due to an ascending infection through a small perforation (for transverse vaginal septum)
Difficulty inserting tampons, persistent bleeding despite tampon placement, tearing of the septum with placement of a tampon or with coitus (for longitudinal vaginal septum)
To facilitate vaginal delivery (for asymptomatic longitudinal vaginal septum)

preauth.label: Detailed Clinical notes with history, indications, symptoms, signs, examination findings and advice for admission

ppd_preauth.q: Detailed Clinical notes – all vitals, detailed history, symptoms, signs, physical examination including local examination, indication for procedure, planned line of treatment and advice for admission?
Did the clinical presentation and composite examination (pelvic examination) confirm the diagnosis?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?
Was the imaging and clinical evaluation indicative of surgery?

common_queries: Clinical notes lack necessary details (history, indications, symptoms, signs, examination findings, planned treatment, or advice for admission).
Clinical presentation and/or composite examination (pelvic examination) did not confirm the diagnosis of vaginal septum.
Imaging (Pelvic/Abdominal USG/MRI) results are missing or do not support the indication for surgery.
Detailed procedure/operative notes are missing or incomplete.
Discharge summary is missing or lacks follow-up advice.
Clinical examination and/or imaging was not indicative of surgery.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Facilities with well-equipped operation theatre, anesthesia and anesthetist availability
clinical_key_pointers: A transverse vaginal septum results when there is failure of fusion and/or canalization of the urogenital sinus and Müllerian ducts and located at various levels in the vagina.
Transverse septa are generally less than 1 centimeter in thickness and may have a small central or eccentric perforation.
The majority of transverse vaginal septa have a fenestration and are thus not completely obstructed.
Longitudinal septa are typically associated with uterine anomalies, such as septate uterus and uterus didelphys.
The septum that divides the vagina may be partial or complete.
surgical_options: Resection of a small, thin transverse septum followed by an end-to-end anastomosis of the upper and lower vaginal mucosa.
Excision of a thick transverse septum (to be attempted only by experienced surgeons due to higher risk of restenosis and obstruction).
Complete resection of longitudinal septum with care to avoid compromise to the bladder and rectum.
